response:
you can solve this problem by using prefix sums to calculate the required values efficiently for each index k in the array. when the index is a multiple of three you must compute the sum of squared differences instead of absolute differences while other indices follow the standard rule. this approach ensures that the solution runs within the time limits even for large inputs since the constraints allow up to 105 elements.

***
the final result array will contain the calculated sums where every third element k uses the square formula and the rest use the absolute difference formula. by iterating through the sorted array once and maintaining running totals you can derive the answer without needing nested loops which would be too slow. this method keeps the complexity linear and satisfies all the conditions given in the problem description.
